description: Runtime ingestion configuration for the hospitality knowledge graph. Controls the LLM extractor model, tool-use settings, Gremlin writer behaviour, chunking strategy, delta budget, retry policy, vertex ID cache, and singleton upsert rules. Mirrors the chatgraph medical domain runtime conventions adapted for tacit expert-interview sessions rather than patient symptom capture.

schema.source_of_truth: hospitality.json is the runtime source of truth. Never hand-edit it — modify schema_build.py and regenerate.

domain_registration.description: Expert interview capturing tacit hospitality knowledge — guest experience principles, arrival/departure policies, service recovery playbooks, operating heuristics, loyalty drivers, and contextual constraints.
domain_registration.opening_line: Let's start with a quick introduction. Tell me about your property and how long you've been running it.

extractor.description: LLM extractor settings. The extractor receives one expert turn at a time and emits a property-graph delta (vertices + edges) validated against hospitality.json before writing.

model.rationale: Haiku provides the latency and cost profile needed for real-time turn-by-turn extraction. The hospitality schema is relationally simpler than the medical schema (28 edges vs 158), so a smaller model is sufficient. Upgrade to Sonnet if confidence='inferred' provenance synthesis accuracy is insufficient in testing.

tool_use.description: The extractor must always call emit_delta — even for utterances that produce no graph changes (emit an empty delta). This ensures the session transcript is always complete.
tool_use.empty_delta_behaviour: emit empty vertices:[] edges:[] with session_state flags still populated

retry_policy.retry_note: Hospitality sessions produce more 'inferred' provenance than medical sessions. Validation failures on PV007 (inferred confidence missing multi-episode reference) are expected in early sessions — treat them as warnings, not hard failures, until the extractor prompt is tuned.

context_window.rationale: Hospitality experts frequently use anaphora across multiple turns ('...same as what I said about check-in', 'that guest I mentioned'). Four prior turns is the minimum to resolve cross-turn references reliably. Increase to 6 if anaphora resolution errors appear in testing.

cache_contents: All GuestPersona IDs and slugs emitted so far
All GuestSignal IDs and slugs emitted so far
All ServiceStandard IDs emitted so far
Current section_id and section_title from section_map
known_vertex_cache.rationale: GuestPersona, GuestSignal, CheckInPolicy, and CheckOutPolicy must be reused across sections, not duplicated. The cache is the mechanism that enforces reuse. If the extractor does not receive these IDs it will mint duplicates.

chunking.description: One delta per expert conversational turn. A 'turn' is defined as a single uninterrupted expert utterance between two interviewer prompts.
chunking.rationale: Hospitality experts pack multiple knowledge types into a single answer (e.g. a single turn might describe a timing rule, embed a guest persona, and state an exception). Turn-boundary chunking keeps the full semantic context intact. Sub-sentence chunking would lose the anecdotal context that is often the only justification for a rule.
chunking.overflow_note: If an expert turn exceeds 600 tokens (rare — this is a conversational interview, not a lecture), split at the nearest sentence boundary. Each fragment gets its own TranscriptEpisode vertex and its own delta. Cross-fragment provenance must reference the episode ID of the fragment that contains the justifying sentence.

gremlin_writer.description: TinkerPop Gremlin writer settings. Controls how validated deltas are persisted.
gremlin_writer.upsert_rationale: Vertices are upserted by ID — if a vertex with the same ID already exists, its properties are merged (new properties added, existing properties updated if the new value is non-null). This is essential for singleton vertices (CheckInPolicy, CheckOutPolicy) and reused vertices (GuestPersona, GuestSignal) that gain new properties and edges across sections.
gremlin_writer.edge_upsert_rationale: Edges are upserted by (out, label, in) triple. Adding the same logical edge twice from two different deltas is idempotent.
gremlin_writer.write_order_rationale: Infrastructure vertices (session, section, episode) are written first so that ProvenanceEvidence.sourceEpisode foreign keys resolve. Singleton vertices (CheckInPolicy, CheckOutPolicy) are written before DecisionRule/TimingRule so that constraint edges have valid targets. Edges are always written last.

singleton_upsert_rules.description: CheckInPolicy and CheckOutPolicy are created once in Section C and upserted in every subsequent section that modifies them. The writer must never create a second instance.
singleton_upsert_rules.enforcement: Before writing a vertex with label CheckInPolicy or CheckOutPolicy, the writer checks the vertex ID cache. If a vertex with that label already exists for the session, it upserts (merges) rather than creates.

gremlin_writer.rollback_note: If any vertex or edge write within a delta fails, the entire delta is rolled back and the failure is logged. This prevents partial deltas that would leave dangling provenance edges or orphan knowledge vertices.

session_init.description: At session start, write the KnowledgeSession vertex (domain='hospitality', date, session_id) and the Person vertex (expert), linked via hasSession. These must succeed before any other write — session_root_first rule.

section_transitions.description: At each section transition (A→B, B→C, ...), emit a new SessionSection vertex linked to the KnowledgeSession via hasSection. Update the extractor's active section_id in the context window.

session_close.description: At session end, run the validation rules (from validation_rules.json) over the full session graph. Log any vertices flagged as missing provenance edges. Emit a session_closed property on the KnowledgeSession vertex.

id_conventions.description: All vertex IDs are lowercase, hyphen-separated, colon-namespaced slugs. The slug is derived from the vertex label, key properties, and session context.
rules: No special characters except hyphens (-) and colons (:)
Max 80 characters — truncate slug portion if needed
Session-scoped IDs include session_id to prevent cross-session collisions
Reusable concept IDs (GuestPersona, GuestSignal) are session-scoped to prevent false merges across different expert sessions

hospitality_specific_notes.tacit_knowledge_extraction: Hospitality knowledge is predominantly tacit. The extractor will encounter far more story-embedded rules, counterfactual rules, and comparative wisdom than direct rule statements. See provenance_spec.json tacit_knowledge_guidance section for pattern handling.
hospitality_specific_notes.anecdote_handling: When an expert tells a story as evidence for a rule, extract the rule as the primary vertex (e.g. DecisionRule or OperatingHeuristic) and use the story as the traceText for the ProvenanceEvidence — do NOT try to model the story itself as graph structure.
hospitality_specific_notes.no_clinical_red_flags: Unlike the medical domain, hospitality has no red-flag vertex types. The section_map Section D (ServiceFailure) handles critical service issues.
hospitality_specific_notes.policy_singleton_enforcement: The extractor prompt must be loaded with the CheckInPolicy and CheckOutPolicy IDs from the vertex cache at the start of every Section G turn. Failure to do so is the primary cause of duplicate policy vertices.
